title: Somalia Parliament Votes to Amend Constitution, Extends Presidential and Legislative Terms
detail: Somalia's parliament voted to amend the provisional constitution, extending the terms of lawmakers and the president from four to five years. The vote proceeded despite a boycott by the opposition alliance. The constitutional amendments were signed into law on March 9 by President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud. Critics — including Puntland, Jubaland, and the Council for the Future of Somalia — condemned the move as a unilateral consolidation of executive power ahead of 2026 elections.

media.caption: Somalia parliament votes to amend constitution, extends terms
